name: Education and Public Institutions Value Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value pack for K-12, public universities, community colleges, research institutions, workforce boards, student services, grants administration, campus safety, and public libraries. Covers $800B+ annual public education spending with focus on enrollment management, student success, research administration, financial aid compliance, and campus operations.

pains.name: Student Information System Fragmentation
pains.description: K-12 districts operate 50+ disconnected systems for SIS, LMS, assessment, IEP, transportation, and food service. Data integration gaps force manual reconciliation and delay intervention decisions.
symptoms: Inability to produce real-time attendance dashboards
State reporting requires 40+ hours of manual prep
sources: Project Unicorn State of Data Interoperability 2023

pains.name: Financial Aid Verification Bottleneck
pains.description: Verification of FAFSA/Title IV eligibility requires manual document review, creating 30-60 day delays. Verification rates >40% strain limited staff and delay disbursement.
symptoms: Return to Title IV calculations error-prone

pains.name: Research Grant Administration Cost Burden
pains.description: Research institutions spend >15% of award value on administrative compliance. Effort reporting, subrecipient monitoring, and NIH/NSF data management plan compliance consume investigator and admin time.

pains.name: Degree Completion & Time-to-Degree Gap
pains.description: 4-year graduation rates at public universities average 52%; community college completion <30%. Students accumulate excess credits, lose aid eligibility, and drop out due to unclear degree paths.

pains.name: Campus Safety & Title IX Compliance Burden
pains.description: Clery Act reporting, Title IX case management, and threat assessment require dedicated systems. Manual processes create reporting delays, compliance gaps, and litigation exposure.
sources: ED Office for Civil Rights Case Resolution Reports

pains.name: K-12 Teacher Shortage & Substitute Crisis
pains.description: Teacher vacancy rates exceed 8% in high-need subjects. Chronic substitute dependency disrupts instruction. Hiring cycles span 90+ days with offer acceptance rates <50%.
sources: Learning Policy Institute Teacher Shortage reports

pains.name: ESSER / Federal Relief Fund Spend-Down Pressure
pains.description: ESSER funds must be obligated by Sept 2024 and liquidated by early 2025. Districts face use-it-or-lose-it pressure with complex documentation and reporting requirements.
symptoms: Unspent ESSER balance >20% at 12 months to deadline

pains.name: LMS / EdTech Sprawl & Data Privacy Exposure
pains.description: Districts and campuses deploy 100+ edtech apps with inconsistent data privacy vetting. FERPA compliance gaps, vendor data breaches, and lack of centralized app governance create risk.
symptoms: Approved app count >100 with no central catalog
Vendor breach notification received in last 12 months
Student data processed in non-US jurisdictions

pains.name: Workforce Board Performance Data Fragmentation
pains.description: WIOA-funded boards must report to DOL on credential attainment, employment placement, and wage gains. Disconnected case management, training provider, and wage record systems create quarterly reporting chaos.
symptoms: Quarterly WIOA report requires 80+ hours manual consolidation

pains.name: Special Education IEP Compliance & Documentation Burden
pains.description: IDEA mandates timely IEP development, progress monitoring, and transition planning. Paper-based processes, therapist scheduling conflicts, and parent communication gaps create compliance risk.
sources: ED Office of Special Education Programs (OSEP)

pains.name: Research Data Management & NIH/NSF Compliance
pains.description: Federal agencies mandate data management plans, public access to publications, and research data preservation. Lack of institutional repositories and metadata standards creates noncompliance risk.
symptoms: DMP approval rate <80% on first submission
Data retention plan missing for 30% of active awards
sources: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy (2023)

pains.name: Library Digital Equity & Broadband Access Gap
pains.description: Public libraries serve as primary broadband access points for 25M+ Americans. Device lending, digital literacy training, and hotspot programs strain capacity with insufficient federal/state support.
symptoms: Hotspot checkout limit reached within 48 hours

pains.name: Enrollment Cliff & Net Tuition Revenue Decline
pains.description: Demographic shifts will reduce traditional college-age population 15% by 2030. Public institutions face simultaneous state funding cuts and declining tuition revenue, forcing program closures.
symptoms: Net tuition revenue per FTE declining
sources: WICHE Knocking at the College Door 2024

pains.name: Student Mental Health Crisis & Counseling Capacity Gap
pains.description: College counseling center demand increased 40% post-pandemic. Waitlists exceed 3 weeks. K-12 social worker ratios exceed 400:1 recommended maximum. Untreated mental health correlates with retention loss.
sources: Center for Collegiate Mental Health Annual Report

pains.name: State Reporting & Federal Compliance Data Chaos
pains.description: States require 200+ data elements for every K-12 student and 100+ for higher ed. Federal reports (IPEDS, CDS, NPSAS, WIOA) have overlapping but non-identical definitions requiring manual reconciliation.
symptoms: State longitudinal data system (SLDS) coverage <80% of required elements
Federal report error rate >5% on first submission
Data certification requires 200+ person-hours per cycle
CIP/SOC code mismatches between SIS and state system

pains.name: Facilities Deferred Maintenance & Energy Inefficiency
pains.description: Public school and campus facilities average 45+ years old. HVAC failures, roof leaks, and energy waste consume 15-25% of operating budgets. Deferred maintenance backlog exceeds $85B nationally.
symptoms: Deferred maintenance backlog >$50M per large district
Energy cost per sq ft >25% above EPA benchmark
HVAC system age >20 years in >60% of buildings
Emergency repair spending >10% of facilities budget
sources: State of Our Schools report (21st Century School Fund)

pains.name: Transfer Credit Articulation & Credit Loss
pains.description: Community college transfer students lose 30-40% of earned credits at 4-year institutions. Non-transparent articulation agreements force retaking courses, extending time-to-degree and exhausting aid eligibility.
symptoms: Average credits lost per transfer student >20
Complaint volume from transfer students increasing

pains.name: Institutional Research & Accreditation Reporting Burden
pains.description: Regional accreditation, state performance funding, and IPEDS reporting require massive institutional research effort. Manual data pulls from disparate systems consume 4,000+ staff hours annually at mid-size universities.
symptoms: Ad hoc reporting request backlog >30 days

kpis.name: Return to Title IV (R2T4) Accuracy Rate
Clery Act Crime Report Timeliness
kpis.benchmarkRange: Statutory: within 3 business days of Oct 1; concerning: >30 days

kpis.name: Title IX Case Resolution Time
kpis.benchmarkRange: ED OCR guidance: resolve promptly; best: <60 days; concerning: >120 days

kpis.name: Emergency Notification System Test Compliance
kpis.benchmarkRange: Clery Act: test annually; best practice: test each semester

kpis.name: ESSER Fund Obligation Rate
kpis.benchmarkRange: Deadline-critical: 100% by Sept 2024; concerning: <80% at 6 months to deadline

kpis.name: WIOA Credential Attainment Rate
kpis.benchmarkRange: DOL threshold: varies by program; best practice: >65%

valueDrivers.name: Student Success & Retention Revenue
valueDrivers.description: Every 1% improvement in retention generates net tuition revenue equal to 0.8% of total enrollment budget at public institutions.

valueDrivers.name: Research Administration Cost Efficiency
valueDrivers.description: Reducing grant admin cost from $150K to $100K per $1M award releases $5M+ annually for a $100M research institution.

valueDrivers.name: Compliance Violation Cost Avoidance
valueDrivers.description: A single ED OCR investigation costs $500K-$2M in legal fees, remediation, and monitoring. Clery Act violations carry civil penalties up to $67K per violation.

valueDrivers.name: Federal Fund Retention
valueDrivers.description: Every 1% of ESSER funds clawed back represents lost investment in recovery. Accelerating closeout releases 3-6 months of working capital for research awards.

valueDrivers.name: Digital Equity & Community Mission
valueDrivers.description: Public libraries and community colleges serve as primary digital access points. Hotspot lending and digital skills training reach populations unreachable by commercial ISPs.

formulas.name: SIS Integration Administrative Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if time-motion study available; MEDIUM if estimated from system logs; LOW if purely speculative

formulas.name: Financial Aid Disbursement Acceleration Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if aid office tracking data available; MEDIUM if using NASFAA averages; LOW if no retention data
formulas.exampleCalculation: 45->15 days, 5K students, 2% retention lift per week saved, $15K net tuition, $200K penalty: 5K*0.02*3*$15K + $200K = $4.7M

formulas.name: Research Grant Admin Cost Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if FDP or institutional time-study data available; MEDIUM if using NCSES averages; LOW if no expenditure data

formulas.name: Degree Completion Revenue Retention Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if institutional retention data available; MEDIUM if using IPEDS peer averages; LOW if no tuition net revenue data
formulas.exampleCalculation: 52%->60%, 4K cohort, $12K net tuition, 4.5 years, $2M program: (0.08*4000*$12K*4.5) - $2M = $15.28M NPV

formulas.name: Title IX / Clery Compliance Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if case management data available; MEDIUM if using Clery Center estimates; LOW if no historical case volume

formulas.name: Teacher Hiring & Retention Cost Savings
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if HRIS and payroll data available; MEDIUM if using Learning Policy Institute estimates; LOW if no staffing data

formulas.name: ESSER Fund Retention Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if district ESSER tracking data available; MEDIUM if using ED dashboard averages; LOW if no balance data

formulas.name: EdTech Privacy & Security Risk Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM if breach history available; LOW if using industry averages ($3.79M education sector IBM 2024); HIGH if app inventory exists

formulas.name: WIOA Performance Reporting Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if current reporting time tracked; MEDIUM if using NAWB estimates; LOW if no performance funding data

formulas.name: Special Education Compliance Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if district special ed data available; MEDIUM if using OSEP estimates; LOW if no complaint history
formulas.exampleCalculation: 5 complaints avoided @$50K, 100 mediation hrs @$150, 30 min reduction * 2K IEPs @$75: $250K + $15K + $75K = $340K

formulas.name: Research Data Management Plan Compliance Value
formulas.confidenceRules: MEDIUM if institutional DMP data available; LOW if no proposal volume data; HIGH if repository cost tracked

formulas.name: Library Digital Equity Program Value
formulas.confidenceRules: LOW (difficult to monetize access); MEDIUM if survey data on primary access usage; HIGH if program cost tracked

formulas.name: Facilities Energy & Maintenance Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH if energy audit and CMMS data available; MEDIUM if using EPA/Energy Star benchmarks; LOW if no utility data

benchmarks.name: K-12 SIS Integration Coverage
benchmarks.source: Project Unicorn State of Data Interoperability 2023

benchmarks.name: ESSER Obligation Rate (National, as of mid-2024)
K-12 School Facilities Deferred Maintenance
benchmarks.source: 21st Century School Fund State of Our Schools

benchmarks.name: Public Library Hotspot Lending Program Reach
Institutional Research Ad Hoc Reporting Backlog

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Duplicate student records >8%; nightly sync failure rate >5%; state reporting error rate >5%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Student information system ecosystem fragmented; data governance immature; intervention decisions delayed; compliance reporting at risk

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Verification queue >500 files; disbursement delays >45 days; student complaint escalation to Ombudsman increasing
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Aid office capacity overwhelmed; verification process manual; Title IV compliance risk; student retention at risk due to delayed funding

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Grant admin FTE >1 per $2M award; effort reporting corrections >20%; closeout backlogs >12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Research administration under-resourced relative to award volume; compliance risk with federal sponsors; investigator dissatisfaction; indirect cost recovery leakage

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Average credits at graduation >140; time-to-degree >5 years for >40% of cohort; stop-out rate >25% in first year
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Degree path unclear; advising insufficient; transfer credit loss; financial aid exhaustion contributing to attrition; revenue loss from unfinished students

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Clery geography mapping incomplete; Title IX response time >60 days; ASR published late; emergency notification untested >6 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Safety infrastructure fragmented; compliance documentation manual; litigation exposure; ED OCR investigation risk; potential for consent decree

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Teacher vacancy rate >8%; substitute fill rate <75%; offer acceptance rate <50%; new teacher turnover >30% in Year 1
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Workforce pipeline broken; instruction quality degraded; emergency credentials dilute standards; HR processes inadequate for market conditions

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Unspent ESSER balance >20% at 12 months to deadline; supplement-not-supplant documentation gaps; procurement compressed into 6-month window
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: District faces clawback risk; compliance documentation inadequate; rushed procurement may violate competitive requirements; ED-OIG audit exposure

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Approved app count >100 with no central catalog; FERPA training completion <50%; vendor breach notification received in last 12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Data governance immature; student privacy at risk; regulatory complaint exposure; district/state liability; parent/community trust erosion

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Quarterly WIOA report requires 80+ hours manual consolidation; wage record matching rate <85%; participant follow-up loss rate >25%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Case management and wage data systems disconnected; DOL performance audit risk; performance funding at risk; administrative waste extreme

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: IEP meetings held past deadline >10%; related service delivery minutes missed >15%; due process complaints increasing
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: IEP process overwhelmed; service provider scheduling broken; parent dissatisfaction rising; state monitoring/complaint intervention likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: DMP approval rate <80% on first submission; data retention plan missing for 30% of active awards; institutional repository adoption <40%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Research data management infrastructure inadequate; NIH/NSF compliance risk; award termination risk; investigator productivity loss

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Patron device waitlist >2 weeks; hotspot checkout limit reached within 48 hours; digital skills class enrollment >capacity by 40%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Library serving as de facto digital safety net with insufficient resources; patron needs unmet; federal digital equity program compliance gap

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: First-time freshman decline >5% annually; net tuition revenue per FTE declining; discount rate >50% for incoming class
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Demographic and competitive pressures eroding revenue base; structural deficit emerging; program rationalization imminent; strategic repositioning required

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Counseling center waitlist >3 weeks; crisis appointments >20% of total; behavioral incident reports increasing 15%+ YoY
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Mental health demand exceeds counseling capacity; retention and completion at risk; liability exposure; potential for tragedy and media attention

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: State longitudinal data system coverage <80%; federal report error rate >5%; data certification requires 200+ person-hours per cycle
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Reporting infrastructure fragmented; manual reconciliation dominant; error correction costs high; state/federal compliance relationship strained

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Deferred maintenance backlog >$50M per large district; energy cost per sq ft >25% above EPA benchmark; HVAC age >20 years in >60% of buildings
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Capital planning underfunded; reactive maintenance dominating; indoor environment degrading; energy waste significant; bond referendum may be needed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Transfer credit acceptance rate <65%; average credits lost per transfer >20; articulation agreement coverage <50% of programs
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Transfer pathway non-transparent; student time-to-degree extended; financial aid eligibility exhausted; community college mission undermined

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Accreditation self-study requires >2,000 hours; IPEDS error rate >3%; ad hoc reporting backlog >30 days; IR staff vacancy rate >15%
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Institutional research capacity insufficient; data democratization lacking; decision-makers waiting for data; accreditation risk from incomplete self-study

evidenceSources.name: NCES IPEDS
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: IPEDS is the definitive source for enrollment, completion, and finance data across all U.S. higher ed institutions.

evidenceSources.name: NCES NPSAS
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: National Postsecondary Student Aid Study provides the most authoritative financial aid and verification rate data.

evidenceSources.name: EDUCAUSE Core Data Service
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Benchmarks IT spending, staffing, and technology adoption across 1,000+ higher ed institutions.

evidenceSources.name: CoSN K-12 Leadership Survey
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: District CTO/CIO perspectives on interoperability, privacy, and digital equity.

evidenceSources.name: NCSES Higher Ed R&D Survey
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Definitive source for research expenditure, funding source, and institutional rankings.

evidenceSources.name: National Student Clearinghouse
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Real-time enrollment and completion tracking across 97% of U.S. college enrollments.

evidenceSources.name: NASFAA Financial Aid Administrator Survey
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Direct operational data from aid offices on processing times, staffing, and technology.

evidenceSources.name: ED.gov ESSER Dashboard
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Real-time obligation and expenditure tracking for all ESSER recipients.

evidenceSources.name: DOL ETA WIOA Performance Reports
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Quarterly performance accountability data for all WIOA areas.

evidenceSources.name: WICHE Knocking at the College Door
evidenceSources.verticalNotes: Definitive demographic projections for college-age population by state through 2037.

buyingTriggers.triggerName: State Longitudinal Data System (SLDS) Upgrade Mandate
buyingTriggers.timingPattern: State legislative sessions + federal SLDS grant cycles (every 3-5 years)
preparatorySignals: State data quality audit findings published

buyingTriggers.triggerName: Title IV Program Review / Audit Finding
buyingTriggers.timingPattern: Post-program review; post-single audit; post-OCR complaint

preparatorySignals: ED ESSER dashboard shows low obligation rate

buyingTriggers.triggerName: Clery Act / Title IX ED Investigation

buyingTriggers.timingPattern: After 2-3 consecutive years of >5% enrollment decline

buyingTriggers.triggerName: State Higher Ed Performance Funding Formula Change
preparatorySignals: Legislative hearing on higher ed funding
Metrics discussion in state higher ed commission

buyingTriggers.timingPattern: E-Rate, NTIA BEAD, IMLS grant cycles

personas.name: University CIO / CTO
personas.role: Chief Information Officer / Chief Technology Officer
personas.organizationType: Public University or State College System
personas.typicalScope: $50M-$200M annual IT budget; 200-2,000 IT staff; 20,000-50,000 students
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for technology architecture; MEDIUM for budget; LOW for academic policy

personas.name: K-12 Superintendent / District Leader
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for district-wide initiatives; MEDIUM for technology procurement; shared with school board
dislikedClaims: Solutions requiring significant teacher retraining without evidence

personas.name: Research Administrator / Sponsored Programs Director
personas.role: Director of Sponsored Programs or Research Administration
personas.organizationType: Public Research University or State Research Institution
personas.typicalScope: $100M-$800M annual research expenditure; 2,000-8,000 active awards; 50-200 admin FTE
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for research admin systems; MEDIUM for compliance; LOW for investigator behavior
dislikedClaims: Generic grant management without federal sponsor specificity

personas.name: Financial Aid Director
personas.organizationType: Public University, Community College, or State System Office
personas.typicalScope: $50M-$300M annual aid disbursement; 5,000-40,000 aid applicants; 20-100 staff
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for aid systems and processes; MEDIUM for compliance; LOW for admissions policy
dislikedClaims: Solutions that don't integrate with COD/NSLDS
Promises of verification automation without document OCR
Vendors unfamiliar with Title IV compliance

personas.name: Registrar / Enrollment Manager
personas.role: University Registrar or Vice Provost for Enrollment Management
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for student systems; MEDIUM for enrollment strategy; LOW for academic curriculum
dislikedClaims: Solutions that don't support reverse transfer
Predictive analytics without demographic bias testing

personas.name: Campus Safety Chief / Clery Compliance Officer
personas.role: Chief of Campus Police / Director of Public Safety / Clery Compliance Coordinator
personas.organizationType: Public University, Community College, or K-12 District
personas.typicalScope: 50-200 sworn/civilian staff; $5M-$25M annual budget; Clery geography across multiple campuses
personas.decisionAuthority: HIGH for safety systems; MEDIUM for compliance; LOW for student conduct policy
trustedEvidence: IAACP (International Association of Campus Law Enforcement)
dislikedClaims: Safety technology without Clery Act integration
AI threat detection without human oversight
Solutions that ignore mental health/counseling coordination

discoveryQuestions.question: How many distinct systems touch your student record data, and what percentage have active API integrations vs. manual file exchanges?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Directly sizing integration gap and manual labor cost

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current state reporting error rate, and how many staff hours are spent on manual data reconciliation before each federal or state submission?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies compliance labor cost and error risk

discoveryQuestions.question: Walk me through your financial aid verification process — how long from document receipt to award notification, and what is your verification selection rate?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Identifies bottleneck and retention risk from delayed disbursement

discoveryQuestions.question: How many research awards are active, and what is the ratio of sponsored programs FTE to annual research expenditure? How does that compare to peer institutions?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Directly measures admin cost burden and recovery efficiency

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your students graduate with more than 120 credits, and what is your average time-to-degree for the most recent cohort?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies revenue loss from excess credits and stop-outs

discoveryQuestions.question: Describe your Clery geography — how many campuses, affiliated properties, and non-campus locations do you track? Is your Annual Security Report published by October 1?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Identifies compliance automation gap and litigation exposure

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current teacher vacancy rate by subject area, and how many days on average does it take to fill a position from posting to first day?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Sizes staffing crisis and instructional disruption cost

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your remaining ESSER balance, and what percentage do you expect to obligate by the federal deadline? What is your biggest obstacle to full spend-down?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Directly quantifies clawback risk and procurement urgency

discoveryQuestions.question: How many third-party educational apps are approved for classroom use, and what percentage have been through a formal privacy and data-sharing review?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies privacy governance gap and breach risk

discoveryQuestions.question: How many hours does your team spend each quarter compiling WIOA performance reports, and what is your current wage record match rate?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Sizes administrative burden and performance funding risk

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of IEPs are held past the regulatory deadline, and how many due process complaints has your district received in the last two years?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies special ed compliance risk and litigation cost

discoveryQuestions.question: How many of your faculty have adopted the institutional repository for publications and data, and what percentage of active grants have compliant Data Management Plans?
What is your current first-time freshman enrollment trend over the last three years, and how has your tuition discount rate changed over the same period?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies enrollment cliff impact on net revenue

discoveryQuestions.question: What is the average wait time for a non-crisis counseling appointment, and what percentage of your counseling appointments are crisis-related?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Identifies mental health capacity gap and retention risk

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current deferred maintenance backlog, and how does your energy cost per square foot compare to EPA Energy Star benchmarks?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Sizes facilities investment gap and energy waste

discoveryQuestions.question: For transfer students, what is the average number of credits that do not apply toward degree requirements, and how many articulation agreements are active?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies transfer revenue loss and mission friction

discoveryQuestions.question: How many person-hours were required for your last accreditation self-study, and how many ad hoc reporting requests does your IR office receive monthly?
What percentage of your student population relies on the library as their primary internet access point, and what is your current device-lending wait time?
discoveryQuestions.valueEngineeringRelevance: Quantifies digital equity gap and unmet demand

objections.objectionText: We just invested in a new SIS/LMS three years ago. We can't justify another technology spend.
objections.responseFramework: Acknowledge investment; reframe as integration/enhancement layer rather than replacement; quantify cost of incomplete integration vs. incremental spend; show peer institutions layering analytics/privacy governance on top of existing SIS

objections.objectionText: Our state procurement process takes 18-24 months. We can't move fast enough for ESSER deadlines.
objections.responseFramework: Identify cooperative purchasing contracts (NASPO, OMNIA, state contracts); map to emergency procurement for clawback risk; quantify daily cost of delay vs. cooperative contract premium; provide pre-negotiated terms

objections.objectionText: We don't have the staff bandwidth to implement a new system right now.
objections.responseFramework: Quantify current manual workaround hours as 'hidden staff bandwidth' already consumed; propose phased implementation with vendor-managed services; show FTE time released post-implementation; reference managed service options

objections.objectionText: Our faculty/instructors won't adopt another new tool.
objections.responseFramework: Position as back-office/administrative improvement, not faculty-facing change; show change management and training included; cite peer adoption rates; offer pilot with volunteer champions

objections.objectionText: Our data is too messy to automate reporting.
objections.responseFramework: Acknowledge data quality as prerequisite; propose data governance assessment as first phase; quantify cost of continuing with messy data (report errors, rework, audit findings); show data cleansing ROI separate from automation ROI

objections.objectionText: We need to see results from a peer institution before we'll consider this.
objections.responseFramework: Provide peer case studies with matched demographics/enrollment; offer site visit or reference call; propose pilot with measurement milestones; leverage state or system office endorsement

objections.objectionText: Title IV / FERPA compliance is too complex to trust to a vendor.
objections.responseFramework: Demonstrate vendor compliance certifications (SOC 2, FERPA training, state audits); show audit trail and documentation features; offer shared liability model; reference existing Title IV-certified implementations

objections.objectionText: We can't get board/trustee approval for capital investment this year.
objections.responseFramework: Reframe as operational expense (SaaS subscription); quantify risk of inaction (clawback, audit findings, enrollment loss); map to existing budget line items being reduced; show payback within fiscal year

objections.objectionText: Our existing vendor says they can add this capability in their next release.
objections.responseFramework: Quantify cost of waiting (missed deadlines, continued manual work, compliance risk); assess vendor roadmap credibility (track record, commitment level); propose parallel path with exit option; show total cost of integrated best-of-breed vs. monolithic delays

technologySystems.name: Student Information System (SIS)
technologySystems.description: Primary system of record for student enrollment, demographics, schedules, grades, and transcripts. Examples: Banner, PeopleSoft Campus Solutions, PowerSchool, Infinite Campus, Skyward.

technologySystems.name: Learning Management System (LMS)
technologySystems.description: Platform for course content, assignments, grading, and communication. Central to hybrid/online instruction.

technologySystems.name: Research Administration System
technologySystems.description: Pre-award (proposal development, routing, compliance) and post-award (accounting, effort reporting, closeout) management.

technologySystems.name: Financial Aid Management System
technologySystems.description: Need analysis, packaging, verification, disbursement, and R2T4 calculations integrated with COD and NSLDS.

technologySystems.name: Degree Audit & Academic Planning
technologySystems.description: Real-time degree progress tracking, what-if scenarios, and transfer credit evaluation.

technologySystems.name: Case Management for Title IX / Conduct
technologySystems.description: Investigation tracking, workflow automation, reporting, and documentation for Title IX, conduct, and Clery incidents.

technologySystems.name: Special Education / IEP Management
technologySystems.description: IEP development, progress monitoring, service delivery tracking, and state reporting for special education compliance.

technologySystems.name: Workforce Development Case Management
technologySystems.description: Participant intake, case notes, service tracking, outcome measurement, and DOL quarterly reporting for WIOA programs.

technologySystems.name: Institutional Research & Analytics Platform
technologySystems.description: Data warehouse, reporting, dashboarding, and predictive analytics for accreditation, IPEDS, and decision support.

technologySystems.name: Emergency Mass Notification System
technologySystems.description: Multi-channel emergency alerting (SMS, email, voice, digital signage, sirens) with geofencing and two-way communication.

technologySystems.name: EdTech App Governance / Privacy Platform
technologySystems.description: App catalog, privacy vetting, data-sharing agreement management, and FERPA compliance tracking for third-party educational tools.

technologySystems.name: Library Management System (ILS)
technologySystems.description: Circulation, cataloging, acquisitions, digital resource management, and patron services.

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Asset / Facilities Management
technologySystems.description: CMMS for preventive maintenance, work orders, space management, and energy tracking across campus/buildings.

technologySystems.name: Admissions / Enrollment CRM
technologySystems.description: Prospect management, application processing, decision support, and yield analytics for enrollment management.

regulatoryFactors.name: FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act)
keyRequirements: Written consent for disclosure of education records
Data breach notification to affected individuals
regulatoryFactors.complianceDeadline: Continuous; breach notification within reasonable time
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Loss of federal funding; ED OCR investigation; individual lawsuits

regulatoryFactors.name: Title IV Student Aid Program Requirements
regulatoryFactors.regulatoryBody: Federal Student Aid / Department of Education
keyRequirements: 90/10 rule for proprietary (relevant for some public sector partnerships)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Loss of Title IV eligibility; fine; provisional certification; Heightened Cash Monitoring

regulatoryFactors.name: Clery Act (Campus Safety and Security)
keyRequirements: Crime statistics for 7 categories across Clery geography
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Civil penalty up to $67,544 per violation; ED program review; reputational damage

regulatoryFactors.name: Title IX (Sex Discrimination in Education)
regulatoryFactors.regulatoryBody: Department of Education Office for Civil Rights
keyRequirements: Grievance procedures for sex discrimination complaints
Response to known acts of sexual harassment
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: ED OCR investigation; loss of federal funding; litigation; consent decree

regulatoryFactors.name: IDEA (Individuals with Disabilities Education Act)
regulatoryFactors.regulatoryBody: Department of Education / State Education Agencies
keyRequirements: Individualized Education Program (IEP) within 30 days of eligibility
regulatoryFactors.complianceDeadline: IEP: 30 days; Annual review: within 1 year; Reevaluation: every 3 years
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Due process complaints; state monitoring; ED OSEP enforcement; compensatory education orders

regulatoryFactors.name: Section 504 / ADA (Disability Accommodation)
keyRequirements: 504 Plan or accommodation plan for qualified individuals
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: ED OCR investigation; DOJ settlement; litigation; consent decree

regulatoryFactors.name: 2 CFR 200 (Uniform Administrative Requirements)
regulatoryFactors.complianceDeadline: Annual single audit; continuous for active awards
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Grant suspension; recovery of funds; debarment; audit findings

regulatoryFactors.name: NIH / NSF Data Management & Public Access Policies
keyRequirements: Data Management and Sharing Plan (NIH, effective 2023)
Public access to publications within 12 months
regulatoryFactors.complianceDeadline: DMS Plan at application; data sharing by end of award or no later than publication
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Award suspension/termination; future funding restriction; institutional reputation damage
sources: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy (2023)

regulatoryFactors.name: ESSER / Federal Relief Fund Compliance
regulatoryFactors.complianceDeadline: ESSER III obligation: Sept 30, 2024; liquidation: varies
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Clawback of unspent funds; ED-OIG audit; GAO review; state withholding

regulatoryFactors.name: WIOA (Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyExposure: Performance funding reduction; DOL monitoring; corrective action; potential decertification

weaknesses: Modernization pace slower than cloud-native competitors
User experience consistently rated below competitors in EDUCAUSE surveys

strengths: LMS market leader in higher ed
weaknesses: Limited SIS integration depth without custom work

strengths: Unified platform expanding into assessment, analytics, special ed
weaknesses: Customer satisfaction gaps in support responsiveness
Core SIS integration still requires middleware

workedExamples.name: K-12 SIS Integration ROI — Mid-Size District
workedExamples.scenarioDescription: A 25,000-student district operates PowerSchool SIS with 75 disconnected edtech apps. State reporting requires 6 FTEs for 3 weeks each quarter. Nightly batch sync failures cause roster errors affecting 8% of students weekly.
calculationSteps: State reporting labor: 6 FTEs * 480 hours/quarter * 4 quarters * $55 = $633,600 annual
Roster error remediation: 25,000 * 0.08 * 0.5 hours * 36 weeks * $55 = $1,980,000 annual
Integration platform investment: $450,000 first year + $180,000 annual
assumptions: Roster error remediation time estimated at 30 minutes per incident

workedExamples.name: University Financial Aid Verification Automation
workedExamples.scenarioDescription: A public university with 15,000 aid applicants has a 42% verification rate. Manual document review creates 38-day average disbursement delay. 12% of verified students drop before disbursement.
calculationSteps: Automated verification reduces delay to 12 days; retention improvement of 4% among verified students
assumptions: 4% retention improvement conservative based on 26-day delay reduction

workedExamples.name: Research Grant Administration Efficiency — R1 University
workedExamples.scenarioDescription: A public R1 university manages $350M in annual research expenditures across 4,200 active awards. Grant admin costs average $145K per $1M awarded. Closeout backlog exceeds 18 months for 30% of expired awards.
calculationSteps: Closeout delay value: 4,200 * 0.30 * $15,000 = $18,900,000 (working capital tied up)
Effort reporting error reduction to 5%: savings $1,181,250
Closeout acceleration to <90 days: recover $12,600,000 working capital
assumptions: Admin cost reduction linear with system investment; actual may vary by discipline
Closeout working capital recovery assumes faster sponsor release

inheritedComponents: Value Driver Framework (6 categories: Mission Effectiveness, Operational Efficiency, Cost Avoidance & Savings, Risk Reduction, Transparency & Accountability, Resource Optimization)
Evidence Source Types (GAO, OIG, budget docs, surveys, etc.)
Signal Source Taxonomy (financial, operational, compliance, external)
Governance Framework (approval, review, confidence, source coverage)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Subpack extends with 5 additional enrollment-related pains (EDU-PAIN-101 through EDU-PAIN-105) covering specific aspects: SIS fragmentation, financial aid processing, research grant admin, degree completion gaps, and campus safety compliance.
Subpack adds vertical specificity for research grants (NIH/NSF), Title IV aid administration, and ESSER/COVID relief compliance with dedicated pains EDU-PAIN-106 through EDU-PAIN-109.
Subpack adds Title IX compliance, Clery Act reporting, mental health crisis response, and K-12 threat assessment specific pains (EDU-PAIN-110 through EDU-PAIN-113).

verticalKPIExtensions: Research Administration Metrics (proposal success, indirect cost recovery)
Financial Aid Metrics (verification rate, disbursement timeliness)
K-12 Operational Metrics (attendance, teacher vacancy, bus on-time)
Library Digital Access Metrics (circulation, program attendance, hotspot lending)
